plan_overview: Refactor paragraph handling so Regular mode uses real paragraph/poetry block containers instead of inferred structure from flat tokens. USFM/Raw modes may stay flat, while downstream operations (export, lint, prettify, search) consume a single canonical flat-token view produced by an adapter.
technical_context: This is a Lexical editor refactor. Regular mode root children become only USFMParagraphNode blocks (type: usfm-paragraph-node) storing the paragraph marker. A new adapter materializeFlatTokensFromSerialized(rootChildren) provides a reading-order flat token list for both flat and tree shapes (including nested editors). Mode switching becomes explicit tree<->flat transforms. Call sites that assumed a built-in paragraph wrapper or relied on USFMTextNode.inPara / data-in-para must be updated.

steps: META depends_on: []

META touches: src/app/domain/editor/nodes/USFMElementNode.ts; src/app/data/editor.ts; src/app/ui/components/blocks/Editor.tsx; src/app/ui/components/blocks/ReferenceEditor.tsx; src/app/ui/components/blocks/NestedEditor.tsx; src/test/helpers/testEditor.ts; (all imports/guards referring to USFMElementNode)
META passes_when: Editor loads with the node registered; Serialized type is "usfm-paragraph-node"; isSerializedElementNode/isSerializedParagraphNode guards updated everywhere
Rename src/app/domain/editor/nodes/USFMElementNode.ts to src/app/domain/editor/nodes/USFMParagraphNode.ts
Rename class USFMElementNode -> USFMParagraphNode and exported type guard helpers accordingly
Change node type constant in src/app/data/editor.ts from USFM_ELEMENT_NODE_TYPE = "usfm-element-node" to USFM_PARAGRAPH_NODE_TYPE = "usfm-paragraph-node"
Update all imports and node registration arrays to reference USFMParagraphNode
Update serialized type unions USFMNodeJSON to use USFMParagraphNodeJSON
Ensure createDOM returns block-ish DOM (existing uses p for marker p, otherwise span; adjust to always block container in Regular mode)
Run unit tests that cover editor creation helpers
